broken_fixed.before_summary: TAG_TENSOR branch returned val_tensor(tensor_shape(args[0]), [].push(0)) — a dummy 1-element tensor — silently discarding the computed `result`. Tensor callers received garbage shape/data; array callers worked fine, so the bug only triggers on the tensor path.
broken_fixed.after_summary: Threads `result` Val-array → raw float vector → val_tensor(shape, raw_out). Pattern now mirrors the working softmax arm (lines 10908-10911) used as reference.

broken_fixed.antipattern: silent_failure / contract_mismatch (duplicate of gelu bug — copy-paste replication)
broken_fixed.before_summary: Same identical antipattern as gelu: TAG_TENSOR path returned val_tensor(shape, [].push(0)) instead of normalized result. Confirms duplicated dispatch antipattern: two arms hit by the same bug, suggesting the original was templated/copied.
broken_fixed.after_summary: Threads `result` through raw_out same as gelu fix. Both arms now share the canonical softmax-style return pattern.

broken_fixed.before_summary: When pos < 0 the `while i <= pos` loop never executes, leaving `scores` empty; the next `let mut max_s = scores[0]` indexes into an empty array (crash or undefined).
broken_fixed.after_summary: Added `if pos < 0 { return val_array([]) }` early-return. NULL-RESULT contract: caller learns no decode happened.

broken_fixed.antipattern: missing_guard (heterogeneous len: ws shorter than outs → ws[e] OOB)
broken_fixed.before_summary: Iterated `e` from 0 to `len(outs)` and accessed `ws[e]` without checking `len(ws)`; if router emits fewer weights than experts, undefined indexing.
broken_fixed.after_summary: Caps inner loop to `min(len(outs), len(ws))` — missing weights are zero-weight (NULL-RESULT for that expert).

notes: softmax (10887): correct dual-path return (used as reference for gelu/normalize fix).
cross_entropy: stable max-subtract softmax, target bounds-checked, returns 0.0 on OOB target (clean NULL-RESULT).
adam_step / adamw_step: bias-correction and eps-stabilized denom; type-safe per-element float coercion.
matmul / matvec / hadamard: explicit dim args, type-coerce both operands, no silent overflow (float accumulators throughout — passes stdlib-5 int-vs-float check).
attention / multi_head_attention / sliding_window_attention: stable softmax (max-subtract), causal mask uses -1e9 sentinel, scale via 1/sqrt(dim).
embedding: 3-arg path bounds-checks `base + dim > len(table)`, returns empty NULL-RESULT.
topk: O(n*k) selection, picks valid sentinel -1 to avoid double-pick.
quantize_int8: zero-range path returns explicit zero array + scale=1.0 (no div-by-zero).
running_stats: Welford-style update; prev_n==0 special-cased to avoid div-by-zero.
log: 1-arg defaults to ln (no silent NaN); 2-arg computes ln(x)/ln(base).
trend_slope / autocorrelation: denom==0 guarded with NULL-RESULT 0.0.
lora_forward: explicit ind/outd/rank, base+=alpha*delta accumulator pattern correct.
rope: handles odd dim with remainder copy.
No exec()/shell-injection arms found in ML range — exec/system calls live elsewhere; ML block is pure compute.

audit_completeness.remainder_tail_arms_unaudited: ~94 arms in tail blocks (consciousness/anima placeholders 12626-12686, Phase-16 FFI memory/JSON 12372-12557, channel/IO/HTTP stubs 13186-end). Most are stub returns (val_void/val_int(0)/val_array([])) — low risk.
audit_completeness.rationale_for_stop: Hit two duplicates of stdlib-3/4/5 antipattern in the actively-used compute arms (gelu, normalize) plus two missing-guard issues (kv_cache_decode, moe_combine). Active-compute coverage of softmax/attention/loss/optimizer/normalization was ~100%; remaining unsampled arms are predominantly stub placeholders that already match NULL-RESULT contract.

antipattern_lineage.stdlib-3-4-5_family: silent failure where dispatch arm returns wrong-shape sentinel rather than computed result OR error

instances_in_stdlib-6.form: val_tensor(shape, [].push(0)) — empty placeholder for tensor-tagged input
identical copy-paste of gelu's broken tensor return

antipattern_lineage.evidence_of_copy_paste: Two arms hit by character-for-character identical buggy expression `val_tensor(tensor_shape(args[0]), [].push(0))` with grep returning exactly 2 matches before the fix and 0 after. Strongly suggests templated dispatch generation that replicated a buggy template.

validation.method: syntactic-only (grep verification + pattern-equivalence with reference softmax arm)
validation.rebuild_skipped_reason: Per session constraint (hexa-only / no shell-orchestrated host build invocation in this cycle); stdlib-5 closure deferred ML block specifically because validation requires E2E selftest — recommend stdlib-7 to E2E-validate.

pattern_equivalence_check.fixed_arm_gelu: lines 11170-11177 — uses identical `for w in result { raw_out.push(w.float_val) } ; return val_tensor(tensor_shape(args[0]), raw_out)` body
pattern_equivalence_check.result: PASS (structurally equivalent to known-good softmax path)

next_recommendation: ω-stdlib-7: E2E selftest of gelu(tensor) and normalize(tensor) — write small hexa probe that constructs a TAG_TENSOR via val_tensor, applies gelu/normalize, and asserts shape preserved + values numerically correct. Without rebuild this cycle could not run dynamic validation.
ω-stdlib-7: audit the remaining ~94 unsampled arms (Phase-16 FFI memory ops + tail stubs). FFI arms are likely candidates for the same family of bugs (silent address-tag mismatches).
ω-stdlib-7: investigate dispatch-template provenance — two character-identical bugs implies a template/macro that needs source-of-truth correction so future generated arms inherit the fix.
ω-stdlib-7: add lint that flags `val_tensor(_, [].push(0))` constructor as forbidden — that exact expression has no valid use case (always evaluates to a 1-element [0]-tagged tensor).

rollback.method: git diff /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/self/hexa_full.hexa shows exactly four hunks (gelu / normalize / kv_cache_decode / moe_combine). Revert with `git checkout -- self/hexa_full.hexa` if regression observed.
rollback.blast_radius: self/hexa_full.hexa only — interpreter source. Until host interp rebuilt from this source the fixes are dormant; previous interp binary unaffected.
rollback.risk_assessment: low — fixes only widen the contract (correct tensor return where dummy was returned; bounds-guard added; no API/type changes). Callers expecting the old buggy single-element tensor would have been broken anyway.
